source: Viasat official incident overview + SentinelOne AcidRain analysis + MITRE ATT&CK S1125 + UK/NCSC and CISA attribution statements
techniques: T1561.001 Disk Wipe Disk Content Wipe
methodological_note: Evidence-based KA-SAT mapping. Initial Access, Lateral Movement, Discovery, and Impact are documented at tactic level. The techniques T1083, T1485, T1561.001, T1529, and T1498 are supported by Viasat, SentinelOne, and MITRE ATT&CK S1125. Persistence is coded false only as a conservative lower-bound in the current binary TCI model; a stricter evidence-status model should mark persistence as unknown. Stealth is coded low because the operation produced overt large-scale service disruption, but no source directly measures stealth; a stricter model should treat stealth as an inferred qualitative judgement rather than a directly documented component.
